perturbation.original_text: 5. RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION Either party may request in writing at any time that any confidential information or any documents containing such confidential information, disclosed pursuant to the terms of this agreement and any copies thereof be returned with a written statement to the effect that upon such return the receiving party has not knowingly r etained in its possession or under its control, either directly or indirectly, any such confidential information or any documents containing such confidential information or any copies thereof and the receiving party shall comply with any such request with in seven days of receipt of such request.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION Either party may request in writing at any time that any confidential information or any documents containing such confidential information, disclosed pursuant to the terms of this agreement and any copies thereof be returned with a written statement to the effect that upon such return the receiving party has not knowingly r etained in its possession or under its control, either directly or indirectly, any such confidential information or any documents containing such confidential information or any copies thereof and the receiving party *shall endeavor* to comply with any such request with in seven days of receipt of such request.
perturbation.explanation: This change weakens the obligation to return confidential information. The original text states that the receiving party 'shall comply' with a return request within seven days. The modified text changes this to 'shall endeavor to comply,' introducing ambiguity. This creates a contradiction if other sections of the agreement assume a strict return policy and rely on that promise. This is a direct contradiction in section 5 itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION Either party may request in writing at any time that any confidential information or any documents containing such confidential information, disclosed pursuant to the terms of this agreement and any copies thereof be returned with a written statement to the effect that upon such return the receiving party has not knowingly r etained in its possession or under its control, either directly or indirectly, any such confidential information or any documents containing such confidential information or any copies thereof and the receiving party *shall endeavor* to comply with any such request with in seven days of receipt of such request.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the receiving party 'shall comply' with a return request within seven days, imposing a strict obligation. The changed text modifies this to 'shall endeavor to comply,' weakening the obligation and introducing ambiguity. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text because it is a direct contradiction in section 5 itself. This creates a direct conflict because the original obligation to 'comply' is replaced with a weaker obligation to 'endeavor to comply', meaning that it is no longer guaranteed they will return the information. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 9. BREACH Should either party (the “defaulting party”) breach any provision of this agreement and fail to remedy such breach within seven days after receiving written notice from the other party (the “aggrieved party”) requiring it to do so, then the aggrieved party shall be entitled, without prejudice to the aggrieved party’s other rights in law, to claim from the defaulting party such damages to which it may be entitled, without prejudi ce to the aggrieved party’s other rights in law, to claim from the defaulting party such damages to which it may be entitled at common law or in equity including inter alia injunction and specific performance. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary cont ained in this agreement, neither party shall be entitled to cancel this agreement in any circumstances whatsoever.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. BREACH Should either party (the “defaulting party”) breach any provision of this agreement and fail to remedy such breach within seven days after receiving written notice from the other party (the “aggrieved party”) requiring it to do so, then the aggrieved party shall be entitled, without prejudice to the aggrieved party’s other rights in law, to claim from the defaulting party such damages to which it may be entitled, without prejudi ce to the aggrieved party’s other rights in law, to claim from the defaulting party such damages to which it may be entitled at common law or in equity including inter alia injunction and specific performance. *However, if the breach is deemed unintentional by an independent arbitrator, the defaulting party will only be liable for direct costs incurred by the aggrieved party*. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary cont ained in this agreement, neither party shall be entitled to cancel this agreement in any circumstances whatsoever.
perturbation.explanation: This adds an exception to the damages clause for unintentional breaches, creating a contradiction. The original text grants the aggrieved party the right to claim damages for any breach. The modified text introduces a scenario where if a breach is deemed unintentional, liability is limited. This creates a contradiction because other sections do not differentiate between intentional and unintentional breaches. This creates ambiguity around the damages that a party may be entitled to. The addition about the 'unintentional breach' undermines the prior established in section 9.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. BREACH Should either party (the “defaulting party”) breach any provision of this agreement and fail to remedy such breach within seven days after receiving written notice from the other party (the “aggrieved party”) requiring it to do so, then the aggrieved party shall be entitled, without prejudice to the aggrieved party’s other rights in law, to claim from the defaulting party such damages to which it may be entitled, without prejudi ce to the aggrieved party’s other rights in law, to claim from the defaulting party such damages to which it may be entitled at common law or in equity including inter alia injunction and specific performance. *However, if the breach is deemed unintentional by an independent arbitrator, the defaulting party will only be liable for direct costs incurred by the aggrieved party*. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary cont ained in this agreement, neither party shall be entitled to cancel this agreement in any circumstances whatsoever.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception for unintentional breaches, limiting liability to direct costs, whereas the original text, and the contradicted section, entitles the aggrieved party to claim full damages for any breach, regardless of intent. This creates a direct conflict within the document regarding the extent of damages recoverable for a breach, as the contradicted section grants entitlement to 'such damages to which it may be entitled at common law or in equity including inter alia injunction and specific performance' while the changed text limits this entitlement if the breach is deemed unintentional. This fulfills the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.
